company_name: Willis Towers Watson Public Limited Company
company_type: Professional Services - Advisory, Broking and Solutions

dependencies: Dependence on third-party service providers and suppliers for business operations
Reliance on technology systems provided by third-party vendors
Dependence on insurance carriers for broking services
Reliance on intercompany lending and funding between subsidiaries
risks: Third-party service providers may fail to perform as expected or services may be disrupted
Failure of third-party vendors to provide technology and support for strategic initiatives
Termination, amendment or consolidation of relationships with insurance carriers could harm business
Concentration of insurance carriers due to industry consolidation
Dependence on limited number of insurance carriers in certain markets

regulatory_risks: Changes to U.S. Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) and Healthcare Reform
Medicare laws and regulations changes
DOL Retirement Security Rule expanding fiduciary standards under ERISA
State and federal insurance brokerage regulations and licensing requirements
HIPAA regulations enforced by Office for Civil Rights
Telephone Consumer Protection Act and telemarketing laws
Medicare Communications and Marketing Guidelines
SEC regulations under Investment Company Act of 1940 and Investment Advisers Act of 1940
ERISA regulations for fiduciary services
Commodities and Futures Trading Commission regulations
Third Party Administrator (TPA) state regulations
U.K. Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulations
Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SMCR) in U.K.
E.U. Insurance Mediation Directive and Insurance Distribution Directive
MiFID II (Markets in Financial Instruments Directive) requirements
E.U. General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
U.K. Data Protection Act
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
U.S. state-level comprehensive privacy laws
E.U. Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
E.U. Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
California Health and Safety Code S.B. 219 on sustainability
SEC climate-related disclosure rules
IFRS Sustainability Standards
Pillar Two global minimum tax of 15% for large multinational companies
OECD Model Global Anti-Base Erosion (GloBE) rules
Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT)
Share repurchase excise tax under IRA
Foreign Account Tax Compliance provisions (FATCA)
Anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act 2010)
Anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing regulations
Antitrust and competition laws globally
Brexit-related regulatory changes and divergence between U.K. and E.U. regulations
FCA authorization requirements for third-country branches post-Brexit
EIOPA supervisory statements on third-country branches

sanction_risks: U.S., E.U., U.K. and U.N. sanctions on Russia due to Ukraine invasion
Sanctions on China and Chinese counter-sanctions
Sanctions on Iran, Cuba, Sudan, Syria, Venezuela
Sanctions on occupied territories of Ukraine (Crimea, Luhansk, Donetsk)
Risk of non-compliance with Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) regulations
Potential for sanctions to disrupt business operations and financial results
Difficulty identifying touchpoints with sanctioned individuals, entities or locations
Increased scope and complexity of sanctions creating compliance risks
Potential for retaliatory cyberattacks and espionage from sanctioned countries
